title: DSDS Foundation Definitions
description: Defines documents for foundations: the broad, cross-cutting design domains a system is built on, such as color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, shape, accessibility, and content. A foundation document holds the identity and metadata of a domain. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries.

foundation.description: The principles, guidelines, scales, and token references that govern a broad design domain across the system, such as color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, accessibility, and content. Foundations are wider than a single visual attribute. A domain may be visual (color, spacing) or cross-cutting (accessibility, content). The top level holds its identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: principles, scale, motion, guidelines, accessibility, content, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a foundation.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the foundation (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation', 'motion', 'shape', 'accessibility', 'content'). MUST be unique within its entity group.

description.description: What this foundation covers, the design domain it governs, and its role in the system. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this foundation, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. It accepts the foundation-specific kinds (principles, scale, motion) plus the general kinds (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters, so tools SHOULD keep it for display. Put internal artifact links (such as token group references) in the `links` metadata field, not in document block entries.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only. This is a separate space that accepts the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation limits, ways to tell confusable entities apart, and machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.
